metrics.narrative: In the 1 Dec 2025 ROI call, Venkata Masetty pressed for specifics on the business case, asking 'Can I expect a 30% efficiency gain, or would it stay something between 20%?' and questioning how sourcing savings of roughly €2.4M were calculated against BMI's addressable spend. Zycus offered benchmark ranges (0.6-1.5% incremental sourcing savings, 30-40% FTE efficiency) but Venkata's own internal business case to management was still being prepared as of that call and has not been confirmed since. The value case is directionally quantified but not yet buyer-owned or signed off; getting Venkata's finished business case and a management decision is the open item.

champion.narrative: Venkata Masetty has been the consistent driver across every touchpoint: he pressed for ROI clarity in the 1 Dec 2025 call, organized and led the 17 Jun 2026 demo (described internally as BMI's 'supply integrated platform' project), and is coordinating BMI's RFP timeline. What has developed: sustained ownership of the evaluation and internal business-case preparation. What is still missing: no evidence of him advocating upward to secure executive/budget sign-off, and no visibility into whether he can move Sharon Moar or other stakeholders to act; Salesforce MEDDPICC (20 Mar 2026) still calls him 'not yet a fully proven internal champion.' He remains developing, not confirmed strong.

competition.narrative: No competitor is actively confirmed in evaluation. Coupa was mentioned in the 17 Jun 2026 demo only as Caroline Lacocque's prior employer, with the note explicitly stating 'no competitor vendors evaluated by BMI stated in meeting' -- context, not competitive intel. Since BMI ran a formal RFP/RFI process, other vendors are almost certainly in play, but none have been named on any call to date. The real competitive risk is an invisible field rather than a defined rival; Zycus's wedge remains its unified single-suite platform and AI/Merlin roadmap, which aligns closely with BMI's stated RFI requirements.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is well articulated and specific: BMI needs to consolidate roughly 40,000 supplier records spread across multiple SAP and non-SAP ERPs, prevent duplicate supplier creation, and support multilingual/regional onboarding (e.g. French, Bulgarian). BMI also flagged bank-account validation as a fraud-risk gap needing Trustpair integration, and wants centralized supplier risk/ESG/performance tracking with SCARs instead of fragmented, inconsistent practices across geographies. Venkata Masetty is the primary pain owner articulating these needs, corroborated by other BMI demo participants during the 17 Jun 2026 session.

paper_process.narrative: No MSA, SOW, or procurement/legal contracting motion has started; the only paper-adjacent item so far is the data-governance and AI-training assurance BMI's legal/IT raised on 17 Jun 2026, requiring written confirmation that Zycus will not train models on BMI data and enforces per-customer segregation. Zycus gave only a verbal answer on the call; no compliance document or DPA-equivalent has been shared. Formalizing that document is the realistic first step toward any paper process, since BMI's legal contact (Dorothee) was already in the room for the demo.

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer or executive sponsor has appeared on any call in the eleven months since Zycus began engaging BMI, and Salesforce MEDDPICC (20 Mar 2026) confirms 'the economic buyer is not yet clearly identified.' Sharon Moar (Group Category Director) is the closest budget-authority contact identified via contact role and attended the 17 Jun 2026 demo, but she did not answer four outbound calls between 22 May and 5 Jun 2026. Venkata Masetty said in the 1 Dec 2025 call he was preparing an internal business case for 'management' approval, but no named executive has since entered the picture. Mapping and directly engaging Sharon Moar (or whoever controls budget) is the critical open action.

decision_process.narrative: The process so far runs discovery (Dec 2025) to RFP submission (8 May 2026) to RFP Q&A/follow-up calls (6 and 28 May 2026, both not recorded) to commercials (3 Jun 2026) to a full solution demo (17 Jun 2026) attended by a wider, cross-functional BMI group including legal (Dorothee) and P2P stakeholders, alongside procurement. That widening attendance signals the evaluation is moving past a single-contact stage, but no named decision-maker, approval chain, or confirmed decision date has surfaced; BMI previously referenced an unnamed executive expected to decide 'early May' with no confirmed update since. The next visibility point is whoever confirms BMI's internal vendor-selection timeline.

decision_criteria.narrative: BMI's RFI/RFP criteria are well documented: a unified, scalable, secure SaaS platform covering supplier vetting/onboarding, risk and compliance, contract management, reporting, and e-sourcing, integrated with ERP and third-party risk data. The 17 Jun 2026 demo tested this directly, with BMI's team probing supplier onboarding gating, duplicate-check logic, ~40,000-supplier ERP consolidation, multilingual support, and Trustpair bank-validation integration. What is not confirmed is how BMI will formally score or weight vendors against these criteria, since no scoring rubric or shortlist status versus other bidders has been shared.

hard.ais_why:
• Dec 2025 Avoma notes from a meeting with BMI's Venkat capture an explicit Zycus action item to 'provide additional materials on Generative AI in procurement and use-case examples from existing clients', plus discussion of Merlin and Teams-based intake. The customer-side ask for GenAI procurement materials and use-case examples is a buyer-originated AI signal, not Zycus rep-led positioning. Merlin and Merlin Intake are in deal scope alongside iSource, iRisk and iContract.
• Formal Evaluation stage with BD Engaged. Venkat is preparing an internal business case for year-end with budget approval pending; commercial discussion captured pricing of USD $220K/yr + $380-400K implementation. ROI workshop and RFP template have been requested by the customer. Last activity May 2026. The deal momentum is anchored in BMI's internal budget-approval cycle ahead of formal RFP.
• High confidence: SF Description was null but Avoma fills the gap decisively with GenAI follow-up requests and an engaged economic buyer (Venkat) preparing a business case. Held at 7 (not 8+) because customer's primary questions are still ROI/efficiency-focused rather than an explicit AI mandate or POC. A buyer-stated 'AI as selection criterion' would push to 8-9.
